paper_title: Late-Holocene Human Resilience in a Fluvial Environment: A Geoarchaeological Dataset for the Central Po Plain (N Italy)

methods.description: Process of constructing a geoarchaeological dataset for the Central Po Plain using heterogeneous sources such as archaeological records, historical maps, documents, geomorphological maps, and terrain surveys. This dataset helps in understanding the Late Holocene landscape evolution.

steps.description: Heterogeneous sources were used to build the dataset here described.
steps.searchable_summary: collect data from various sources including archaeological records, historical maps, and geomorphological maps

steps.description: In particular, archaeological records, historical maps and documents and geomorphological maps served as a useful starting point to construct the dataset in association with terrain surveys.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate data from archaeological records, historical maps, and geomorphological maps with terrain surveys

steps.description: The combination of geosciences data and archaeo-historical records were fundamental in understanding the Late Holocene landscape evolution of this portion of the Po Plain.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze combined geosciences and archaeo-historical data to understand landscape evolution
